Traffic Manager delivers global traffic distribution with latency-based optimization.

Reference: Traffic Manager routing methods

questions.promptHtml: Network team needs to investigate connectivity issues between VM-A (10.0.1.4) and VM-B (10.0.2.4) in same VNet. Solution must diagnose NSG rules evaluation, effective routes, and packet flow analysis showing where traffic is blocked.

options.label: Use Network Watcher IP flow verify and NSG diagnostics to test connectivity, then Next hop to verify routing decisions
Enable NSG flow logs and query Log Analytics
Use ping and traceroute utilities from VM

questions.correctLabel: Use Network Watcher IP flow verify and NSG diagnostics to test connectivity, then Next hop to verify routing decisions
questions.extraHtml:
Network Watcher IP flow verify tests 5-tuple (source/dest IP:port, protocol) showing if traffic is allowed/denied by NSG rules with specific rule identification. NSG diagnostics shows effective security rules. Next hop identifies routing decisions (system route, UDR, VNet peering). NSG flow logs provide historical data not live troubleshooting. Ping/traceroute don't show NSG/route evaluation details. Network Watcher provides comprehensive network path analysis.

Reference: IP flow verify overview

questions.promptHtml: A storage account contains critical compliance data that must not be modified or deleted for 7 years per regulatory requirements. Administrators with Contributor access must also be unable to delete data. Solution must prevent bypassing at storage account level.

options.label: Configure immutability policy (legal hold or time-based retention) on blob containers with locked state preventing policy deletion
Use CanNotDelete resource lock on storage account
Enable blob soft delete with 2555-day retention

questions.correctLabel: Configure immutability policy (legal hold or time-based retention) on blob containers with locked state preventing policy deletion
questions.extraHtml:
Immutability policies (WORM - Write Once Read Many) make blobs immutable (no modification/deletion) for specified retention period or legal hold. Locked policies prevent policy deletion even by account admins. CanNotDelete lock prevents account deletion but not blob modification. Soft delete provides recovery window (up to 365 days) but doesn't prevent deletion. Immutability policies enforce regulatory compliance with cryptographic guarantees.

Reference: Immutable storage for blob data

questions.promptHtml: A storage account experiences accidental blob deletions weekly. Solution must enable recovery of deleted blobs for 30 days while maintaining previous versions of modified blobs for rollback. Minimize storage costs for version history.

options.label: Enable blob soft delete (30 days) and blob versioning with lifecycle management policy deleting old versions after 90 days
Use Azure Backup for storage account
Take daily blob snapshots manually

questions.correctLabel: Enable blob soft delete (30 days) and blob versioning with lifecycle management policy deleting old versions after 90 days
questions.extraHtml:
Blob soft delete retains deleted blobs for specified period (up to 365 days) enabling undelete operations. Blob versioning automatically preserves previous versions on each overwrite. Lifecycle policies transition or delete old versions to control costs. Azure Backup doesn't support blob-level recovery. Manual snapshots require operational overhead. Soft delete + versioning + lifecycle provides comprehensive data protection with cost optimization.

Reference: Soft delete for blobs

questions.promptHtml: A financial application requires VM disk encryption with customer control over encryption keys, key rotation logging, and ability to revoke access immediately preventing VM startup without key access.

options.label: Enable Azure Disk Encryption with customer-managed keys in Azure Key Vault Premium (HSM), configure Key Vault access policies with VM managed identity, enable Key Vault audit logging
Use server-side encryption with platform-managed keys
Enable BitLocker with local key management

questions.correctLabel: Enable Azure Disk Encryption with customer-managed keys in Azure Key Vault Premium (HSM), configure Key Vault access policies with VM managed identity, enable Key Vault audit logging
questions.extraHtml:
Customer-managed keys (CMK) in Key Vault provide key control (rotation, revocation, audit). Key Vault Premium uses FIPS 140-2 Level 3 HSMs. VM managed identity accesses keys via access policies. Key revocation prevents VM startup (key wrapping at boot). Platform-managed keys don't provide customer control. Local BitLocker lacks centralized management and audit. CMK + Key Vault delivers enterprise key management with compliance logging.

Reference: Create and configure Key Vault for Azure Disk Encryption

questions.promptHtml: A VM requires maximum IOPS (160,000+) and throughput (10 GB/s) for high-performance database workload with sub-millisecond latency requirements. Data disk size is 8TB.

options.label: Attach Ultra Disk with configurable IOPS and throughput independent of disk size, enabling ultra disk compatibility on VM SKU
Deploy Premium SSD v2 with maximum provisioned IOPS
Use multiple Premium SSD disks in storage pool

questions.correctLabel: Attach Ultra Disk with configurable IOPS and throughput independent of disk size, enabling ultra disk compatibility on VM SKU
questions.extraHtml:
Ultra Disks provide highest performance (up to 160K IOPS, 10 GB/s per disk) with sub-millisecond latency and independent IOPS/throughput settings separate from capacity. Requires VM SKU support and ultra disk capability enablement. Premium SSD v2 offers up to 80K IOPS (lower than Ultra). Storage spaces with Premium SSD adds complexity and doesn't match single Ultra Disk performance. Ultra Disk delivers extreme performance for latency-sensitive workloads.

Reference: Ultra Disks

questions.promptHtml: Development team needs consistent VM disk snapshots including data in-memory and pending I/O operations for application-consistent backup of multi-disk configurations (OS + 5 data disks) for SQL Server VM.

options.label: Use Azure Backup for VM which orchestrates VSS quiescing creating application-consistent snapshot across all disks atomically
Create incremental snapshots of each managed disk individually via Azure CLI
Enable geo-redundant storage on managed disks

questions.correctLabel: Use Azure Backup for VM which orchestrates VSS quiescing creating application-consistent snapshot across all disks atomically
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Backup coordinates with VSS (Volume Shadow Copy Service) to quiesce applications (SQL Server, Exchange) ensuring consistent state across all VM disks in single snapshot operation. Individual disk snapshots are crash-consistent only (no app awareness or multi-disk atomicity). GRS is replication type not snapshot mechanism. Azure Backup provides application-aware, multi-disk consistent backup.

Reference: Snapshot consistency

questions.promptHtml: Two clustered VMs (SQL Server Always On) require shared block storage accessible simultaneously from both nodes for witness disk with SCSI persistent reservations support.

options.label: Deploy Azure Shared Disk (Premium SSD or Ultra Disk) with maxShares=2 and attach to both VMs in cluster
Use Azure Files Premium share with SMB 3.0
Configure disk replication between two independently attached disks

questions.correctLabel: Deploy Azure Shared Disk (Premium SSD or Ultra Disk) with maxShares=2 and attach to both VMs in cluster
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Shared Disks enable multiple VMs to attach same managed disk concurrently (maxShares up to 5/100 depending on tier) with SCSI-3 persistent reservations for cluster coordination. Required for Windows Server Failover Clustering (WSFC) shared storage scenarios. Azure Files SMB doesn't provide block-level SCSI reservations. Independent disks lack shared access. Shared Disks deliver clustered storage for Windows/Linux clusters.

Reference: Azure shared disks

questions.promptHtml: An organization requires keeping VM OS up-to-date with Windows security patches and critical updates automatically across 500 VMs in multiple subscriptions. Solution must support maintenance windows (2AM-6AM weekends) and pre/post patching scripts.

options.label: Configure Azure Update Management (or Azure Update Manager) with update deployment schedule, maintenance window, pre/post scripts, and dynamic groups targeting VMs by tag
Enable Windows Update automatic updates on each VM
Use WSUS server in Azure

questions.correctLabel: Configure Azure Update Management (or Azure Update Manager) with update deployment schedule, maintenance window, pre/post scripts, and dynamic groups targeting VMs by tag
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Update Management/Manager provides centralized patch orchestration with scheduled deployments, maintenance windows, reboot control, pre/post-patching scripts (PowerShell/Python), and dynamic VM targeting by tag/subscription. Native Windows Update lacks centralized management and reporting. WSUS requires infrastructure maintenance and doesn't support Azure-aware targeting. Update Management delivers enterprise patch automation at cloud scale.

Reference: Azure Update Manager overview

questions.promptHtml: A subscription has over-provisioned VMs (CPU <10%), underutilized storage accounts, and unattached disks costing $15K/month. Finance requires automated recommendations for cost optimization and performance improvement.

options.label: Review Azure Advisor recommendations filtered by Cost and Performance categories and implement suggested actions (VM rightsizing, delete unattached disks, enable reserved instances)
Manually review Azure Cost Management cost analysis
Deploy third-party monitoring solution

questions.correctLabel: Review Azure Advisor recommendations filtered by Cost and Performance categories and implement suggested actions (VM rightsizing, delete unattached disks, enable reserved instances)
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Advisor provides personalized best practice recommendations across Cost, Performance, Security, Reliability, and Operational Excellence. Analyzes resource configurations and usage telemetry (CPU, memory, disk) suggesting specific actions (VM size changes, reserved capacity, orphaned resources). Cost Management shows spending trends but doesn't provide prescriptive recommendations. Third-party tools add cost. Advisor delivers free, Azure-native optimization guidance.

Reference: Azure Advisor overview

questions.promptHtml: An infrastructure-as-code team must deploy 50 identical resource groups across subscriptions, each containing VNet, NSG, Storage Account, VM, and Key Vault with parameterized naming and SKUs. Solution must support validation before deployment and rollback on failure.

options.label: Create Azure Resource Manager (ARM) template or Bicep file with parameters, use what-if operation for validation, deploy using deployment modes (complete/incremental)
Deploy resources manually via Portal with documented steps
Use Azure CLI commands in bash script

questions.correctLabel: Create Azure Resource Manager (ARM) template or Bicep file with parameters, use what-if operation for validation, deploy using deployment modes (complete/incremental)
questions.extraHtml:
ARM templates/Bicep provide declarative infrastructure-as-code with parameterization (names, SKUs, locations), dependency management, validation (what-if shows changes), and atomic deployment (all-or-nothing with automatic rollback on error). Portal deployment isn't repeatable or versionable. CLI scripts are imperative (require error handling logic). ARM templates deliver idempotent, validated, auditable infrastructure deployment.

Reference: ARM templates overview

questions.promptHtml: A microservices application (20 services) requires container orchestration with automatic scaling, service discovery, rolling updates, and integrated Azure services (Azure AD, Key Vault, Azure Monitor). Development team familiar with Kubernetes.

options.label: Deploy Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) cluster with managed identity, Azure AD integration, Azure Key Vault provider for Secrets Store CSI driver, and Container Insights
Use Azure Container Instances with manual networking
Deploy Docker Swarm on VMs

questions.correctLabel: Deploy Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) cluster with managed identity, Azure AD integration, Azure Key Vault provider for Secrets Store CSI driver, and Container Insights
questions.extraHtml:
AKS provides managed Kubernetes with Azure integration including Azure AD RBAC (cluster access), managed identity (keyless auth), Key Vault CSI driver (secret injection), Container Insights (monitoring), and auto-scaling (HPA, cluster autoscaler). ACI lacks orchestration features for complex microservices. Swarm on VMs requires undifferentiated infrastructure management. AKS delivers enterprise Kubernetes with Azure-native integrations.

Reference: Azure Kubernetes Service overview

questions.promptHtml: A regulatory audit requires proof that administrator accessed encrypted VM disk contents at specific timestamp. Audit trail must show key access events and identity of accessor.

options.label: Enable Azure Key Vault diagnostic logs forwarding to Log Analytics workspace and query AzureDiagnostics table for KeyVaultKeySecurityEventOperation events with caller identity
Review Azure Activity Log for VM operations
Enable VM boot diagnostics

questions.correctLabel: Enable Azure Key Vault diagnostic logs forwarding to Log Analytics workspace and query AzureDiagnostics table for KeyVaultKeySecurityEventOperation events with caller identity
questions.extraHtml:
Key Vault diagnostic logs capture all key operations (Get, Wrap, Unwrap) including caller identity (Azure AD user/service principal), timestamp, IP address, and operation result. Disk encryption wraps/unwraps DEKs using KEKs stored in Key Vault. Activity Log shows management operations (VM start/stop) not key access. Boot diagnostics show console output. Key Vault audit logs provide cryptographic operation audit trail for compliance.

Reference: Azure Key Vault logging

questions.promptHtml: A storage account receives 1 million blob upload requests per day (1MB each). Application performs mostly sequential writes. Storage costs are acceptable but transaction costs ($0.0004 per 10K transactions) accumulate to $40/day. Minimize transaction costs without sacrificing write performance.

options.label: Use Premium Block Blob storage account with flat-rate pricing (no per-transaction charges) for write-intensive workloads
Use Standard storage Hot tier with request optimization
Enable blob access tier optimization policies

questions.correctLabel: Use Premium Block Blob storage account with flat-rate pricing (no per-transaction charges) for write-intensive workloads
questions.extraHtml:
Premium Block Blob storage uses performance-based pricing (per-GB and provisioned capacity) without per-transaction charges, optimizing costs for high-transaction workloads (reads/writes). Standard storage tiers charge per-transaction (Hot: $0.0004/10K writes adds up quickly at scale). No requestoptimization or tier optimization features exist. Premium Block Blob eliminates transaction cost concerns for write-heavy scenarios while maintaining performance.

Reference: Premium performance for block blobs

questions.promptHtml: An application must trigger automatic remediation workflows when virtual machine CPU exceeds 90% for 15 minutes. The solution requires sending notifications to multiple channels (email, SMS, webhook) and executing an Azure Automation runbook to scale resources. All alerting must target 200 VMs across 5 subscriptions dynamically.

options.label: Create metric alert rule with dynamic threshold, action group targeting email/SMS/webhook/runbook, and scope to multiple subscriptions using Azure Resource Graph query
Configure individual VM alerts with static thresholds
Use Azure Monitor Logs alert with manual VM list

questions.correctLabel: Create metric alert rule with dynamic threshold, action group targeting email/SMS/webhook/runbook, and scope to multiple subscriptions using Azure Resource Graph query
questions.extraHtml:
Metric alert rules support multi-resource scopes (query-based targeting), dynamic thresholds (machine learning-based baselines), and action groups (orchestrate multiple notification channels and automation runbooks). Individual VM alerts don't scale (require 200 separate alerts). Log alerts add query latency. Metric alerts with action groups provide real-time monitoring with multi-channel automated response.

Reference: Metric alerts in Azure Monitor

questions.promptHtml: Security team requires visibility into network traffic patterns for all VMs in production subnet (10.0.1.0/24) including source/destination IPs, ports, protocols, and NSG rule decisions. Data must be retained for 90 days and queryable using KQL for threat investigation.

options.label: Enable NSG flow logs version 2 with traffic analytics, configure Log Analytics workspace storage, and use Traffic Analytics dashboard and KQL queries
Configure packet capture on each VM using Network Watcher
Export NSG diagnostic logs to Event Hub

questions.correctLabel: Enable NSG flow logs version 2 with traffic analytics, configure Log Analytics workspace storage, and use Traffic Analytics dashboard and KQL queries
questions.extraHtml:
NSG flow logs capture all traffic (allowed/denied) with 5-tuple details and NSG rule evaluation results. Version 2 adds flow state and throughput metrics. Traffic Analytics provides visualization and threat intelligence. Log Analytics enables KQL querying (NetworkSecurityGroupFlowEvents table). Packet capture is short-duration troubleshooting tool (not continuous monitoring). Event Hub lacks query interface. NSG flow logs + Traffic Analytics deliver network forensics with long-term retention.

Reference: NSG flow logs overview

questions.promptHtml: Infrastructure audit requires generating a report showing all VMs with Premium SSD disks, their locations, associated NSGs, and total monthly cost across 50 subscriptions and 10 management groups. Report must refresh automatically and export to CSV.

options.label: Create Azure Resource Graph query using KQL to join resources, compute, and cost data, schedule query with Azure Logic Apps or Automation, export results
Use Azure Cost Management cost analysis with filters
Manually query each subscription via PowerShell

questions.correctLabel: Create Azure Resource Graph query using KQL to join resources, compute, and cost data, schedule query with Azure Logic Apps or Automation, export results
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Resource Graph provides millisecond-latency queries across subscriptions/management groups using KQL. Queries span resource properties, relationships (NSG assignments), and cost data. Supports JOIN operations for complex relationships. Cost Management focuses on cost only (lacks resource property joins). Manual PowerShell scripts don't scale (API throttling, slow). Resource Graph delivers enterprise-scale resource inventory and reporting.

Reference: Azure Resource Graph overview

questions.promptHtml: A departmental subscription has $50K monthly budget. Finance requires automatic email alerts at 80% spend, automatic webhook notification at 90% to trigger cost optimization runbook, and spending must be capped preventing new resource creation at 100%.

options.label: Configure Azure Budget with 80% and 90% alert thresholds targeting action groups (email + webhook), but understand Azure Budgets don't enforce spending caps - use Azure Policy Deny effects for preventive controls
Use Azure Cost Management with automatic resource shutdown
Enable subscription spending limits

questions.correctLabel: Configure Azure Budget with 80% and 90% alert thresholds targeting action groups (email + webhook), but understand Azure Budgets don't enforce spending caps - use Azure Policy Deny effects for preventive controls
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Budgets provide threshold-based alerts (percentage/absolute amounts) with action group integration (email, SMS, webhook, Logic Apps). Budgets are informational only (don't prevent spending). Spending limits only available for credit-based subscriptions (Visual Studio, Azure Pass). Azure Policy Deny effects can prevent specific resource creation but don't enforce dollar amounts. Budget + action groups + Policy provide cost governance with proactive alerting.

Reference: Create and manage budgets

questions.promptHtml: An organization requires centralized DNS resolution for custom domain (internal.contoso.com) across 80 Azure VNets and multiple on-premises datacenters. DNS records must be managed centrally with automatic VM registration for Azure resources.

options.label: Create Azure Private DNS zone for internal.contoso.com, enable auto-registration for VNet links, deploy Azure DNS Private Resolver for hybrid connectivity
Deploy DNS server VMs manually in hub VNet with conditional forwarding
Use Azure Public DNS zones with VNet links

questions.correctLabel: Create Azure Private DNS zone for internal.contoso.com, enable auto-registration for VNet links, deploy Azure DNS Private Resolver for hybrid connectivity
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Private DNS zones provide managed DNS hosting for private domains with auto-registration (automatically creates A/AAAA records when VMs are created/deleted in linked VNets). VNet links enable resolution across all linked VNets. DNS Private Resolver bridges Azure and on-premises DNS. DNS server VMs require management and lack auto-registration. Public DNS zones are internet-accessible. Private DNS + resolver delivers zero-maintenance hybrid DNS.

Reference: Azure Private DNS overview

questions.promptHtml: A multi-tier application requires that web tier VMs can access Azure SQL Database and Azure Key Vault without allowing access to internet or other Azure services. NSG rules must minimize management overhead by avoiding hardcoded IP addresses.

options.label: Create NSG rules using service tags (Sql, KeyVault) in destination allowing port 1433/443, with default DenyAllOutbound rule
Configure NSG with specific Azure datacenter IP ranges
Use application security groups for Azure PaaS services

questions.correctLabel: Create NSG rules using service tags (Sql, KeyVault) in destination allowing port 1433/443, with default DenyAllOutbound rule
questions.extraHtml:
Service tags represent Azure service IP ranges (Sql = all Azure SQL IPs in region, KeyVault = all Key Vault IPs). Azure maintains service tag IP ranges automatically (no manual updates needed). Supports regional/global scoping. Hardcoded IP ranges require constant updates as Azure services scale. ASGs group VMs not PaaS services. Service tags simplify NSG rules for Azure service connectivity without IP management.

Reference: Service tags overview

questions.promptHtml: An Azure Load Balancer Standard SKU distributes traffic across 10 backend VMs. Administrators report that traffic is unevenly distributed with 3 VMs handling 80% of connections. Health probes show all VMs healthy. Load balancer must distribute new connections evenly.

options.label: Change load distribution mode from 5-tuple hash to source IP affinity or configure session persistence timeout based on requirements
Add more backend VMs
Upgrade to Application Gateway

questions.correctLabel: Change load distribution mode from 5-tuple hash to source IP affinity or configure session persistence timeout based on requirements
questions.extraHtml:
Load Balancer distribution algorithms: 5-tuple hash (default, source IP/port + dest IP/port + protocol) creates sticky sessions for same client/port combinations. Source IP affinity (2-tuple or 3-tuple) distributes based on client IP only. Uneven distribution often indicates persistent connections from few clients. Adjusting distribution mode or connection draining settings balances load. Adding VMs doesn't fix distribution algorithm. Application Gateway is Layer 7 (not required). Understanding distribution algorithms resolves connection imbalance.

Reference: Load Balancer distribution modes

questions.promptHtml: A web application behind Application Gateway experiences intermittent timeout errors for specific API endpoints that perform long-running operations (5-10 minutes). Other endpoints work normally. Application Gateway must support these long operations without timing out.

options.label: Increase backend HTTP timeout settings on Application Gateway to 600 seconds, adjust backend connection draining timeout, and optimize backend API for async patterns
Scale Application Gateway to higher SKU
Configure health probe intervals to 300 seconds

questions.correctLabel: Increase backend HTTP timeout settings on Application Gateway to 600 seconds, adjust backend connection draining timeout, and optimize backend API for async patterns
questions.extraHtml:
Application Gateway default backend connection timeout is 30 seconds (configurable up to 86,400s). Backend timeout must exceed longest-running operation. Health probe intervals are separate from connection timeouts (probes check availability, not operation duration). Scaling SKU increases capacity not timeout limits. Long-running synchronous APIs indicate design antipattern (consider async request-reply pattern with status polling). Timeout configuration accommodates legacy APIs while async patterns provide better architecture.

Reference: Configure HTTP settings

questions.promptHtml: An Application Gateway must inspect and block SQL injection attacks, cross-site scripting, and other OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities in HTTP traffic to backend web applications. Solution must update threat signatures automatically.

options.label: Enable Web Application Firewall (WAF) on Application Gateway with OWASP rule set, configure detection/prevention mode, enable managed rule set auto-updates
Configure NSG rules blocking malicious patterns
Implement input validation in application code only

questions.correctLabel: Enable Web Application Firewall (WAF) on Application Gateway with OWASP rule set, configure detection/prevention mode, enable managed rule set auto-updates
questions.extraHtml:
Application Gateway WAF provides Layer 7 threat protection with OWASP ModSecurity Core Rule Set (CRS). Detection mode logs threats, prevention mode blocks. Managed rule sets auto-update with new threat signatures. Supports custom rules and exception lists. NSG is Layer 4 (can't inspect HTTP payloads). Application-level validation is last line of defense (should be layered with gateway WAF). WAF provides centralized web application protection with zero application changes.

Reference: WAF on Application Gateway

questions.promptHtml: A VM fails to boot and administrators cannot RDP/SSH. Azure Portal shows VM Status as 'Running' but OS is unresponsive. Administrators need to view boot sequence logs and interact with VM without network connectivity to diagnose boot failures.

options.label: Enable boot diagnostics (stores screenshots and serial console logs) and use Azure Serial Console for direct VM access via serial port
Detach OS disk and attach to another VM for analysis
Force restart VM multiple times

questions.correctLabel: Enable boot diagnostics (stores screenshots and serial console logs) and use Azure Serial Console for direct VM access via serial port
questions.extraHtml:
Boot diagnostics capture VM boot screenshots (visual boot state) and serial console output (boot logs) in storage account. Azure Serial Console provides out-of-band access to VM via serial port (SAC/GRUB) independent of networking, allowing single-user mode, bootloader reconfiguration, and kernel parameter changes. OS disk analysis requires VM downtime and complex steps. Force restart doesn't provide diagnostic data. Boot diagnostics + Serial Console enable low-level VM troubleshooting.

Reference: Boot diagnostics

questions.promptHtml: An Azure Automation runbook must perform VM operations (start/stop/restart) across 300 VMs in multiple subscriptions. The runbook must authenticate securely without storing credentials and follow least-privilege principle.

options.label: Use Automation Account system-assigned managed identity or Run As account (deprecated), grant specific RBAC roles (VM Contributor) at resource group or subscription scope
Store Azure AD credentials in Automation variables
Use certificate-based service principal authentication with Key Vault

questions.correctLabel: Use Automation Account system-assigned managed identity or Run As account (deprecated), grant specific RBAC roles (VM Contributor) at resource group or subscription scope
questions.extraHtml:
Automation Accounts support system-assigned and user-assigned managed identities (preferred over deprecated Run As accounts). Managed identity eliminates credential management (tokens auto-rotate). Assign minimum required permissions (Virtual Machine Contributor for start/stop, avoid Contributor). Automation variables encryption is insufficient for credentials. Certificate-based auth requires certificate lifecycle management. Managed identity delivers keyless, auditable automation authentication.

Reference: Automation security overview

questions.promptHtml: A critical application requires point-in-time recovery within 5 minutes of accidental file deletion on Azure File share (1TB data). Solution must restore individual files without recovering entire share and support retention for 30 days.

options.label: Enable Azure Backup for Azure Files with hourly snapshot schedule, use item-level restore for individual file recovery
Configure manual share snapshots via Storage Account
Use blob soft delete

questions.correctLabel: Enable Azure Backup for Azure Files with hourly snapshot schedule, use item-level restore for individual file recovery
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Backup for Azure Files uses share snapshots with scheduled policies (hourly, daily), retention up to 9999 days, and item-level restore (individual files/folders) from any snapshot. Manual snapshots require operational overhead and don't provide scheduled automation. Blob soft delete applies to blobs not file shares (different storage services). Azure Backup delivers automated, policy-driven file share protection with granular recovery.

Reference: Azure file share backup

questions.promptHtml: An on-premises file server (Windows Server 2019) stores 5TB of departmental files accessed by 200 users. Company requires cloud backup retention (7 years), disaster recovery capability, and gradual cloud migration without immediate cutover. Solution must maintain on-premises file access during migration.

options.label: Deploy Azure Backup agent (MARS) for file-level backup to Recovery Services vault, then migrate to Azure File Sync for hybrid file access and cloud tiering
Use Azure Site Recovery to replicate entire server VM
Manually copy files to Azure Blob Storage via AzCopy

questions.correctLabel: Deploy Azure Backup agent (MARS) for file-level backup to Recovery Services vault, then migrate to Azure File Sync for hybrid file access and cloud tiering
questions.extraHtml:
Microsoft Azure Recovery Services (MARS) agent provides file/folder-level backup to Azure with long-term retention (up to 9999 days), supports BMR (bare metal recovery). Azure File Sync enables hybrid file server (on-premises cache + cloud storage) with automatic tiering. Migration path: MARS for immediate backup protection → File Sync for cloud migration. ASR replicates VMs not files specifically. AzCopy is manual without retention policies. MARS + File Sync delivers backup-first migration strategy.

Reference: MARS agent overview

questions.promptHtml: A disaster recovery plan requires testing failover of 20 production VMs to DR region without impacting production environment. Test must validate network configurations, VM connectivity, and application functionality in isolated network.

options.label: Execute Azure Site Recovery test failover with isolated test virtual network, validate functionality, clean up test resources without committing failover
Perform planned failover with immediate failback
Create network traffic mirroring to DR region

questions.correctLabel: Execute Azure Site Recovery test failover with isolated test virtual network, validate functionality, clean up test resources without committing failover
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Site Recovery test failover creates VMs in isolated VNet (doesn't affect replication or production), validates recovery plans without data loss risk, and allows DR readiness testing. Cleanup removes test resources automatically. Planned failover affects production (commits failover, requires failback). Traffic mirroring doesn't validate DR capability. Test failover enables zero-impact DR validation with complete cleanup.

Reference: Test failover to Azure

questions.promptHtml: A VM Scale Set uses Manual orchestration mode. New application version must be deployed to all 50 instances with phased rollout (10 instances at a time) and automatic rollback if health checks fail. Existing connections must drain gracefully during upgrades.

options.label: Convert to Uniform orchestration mode or use Flexible with upgrade policy (Manual → Rolling), enable Application Health extension, configure rolling upgrade policy with batch size 10
Manually update each VM instance using Custom Script Extension
Delete and recreate scale set with new image

questions.correctLabel: Convert to Uniform orchestration mode or use Flexible with upgrade policy (Manual → Rolling), enable Application Health extension, configure rolling upgrade policy with batch size 10
questions.extraHtml:
Uniform orchestration mode supports rolling upgrades with configurable batch size, pause between batches, automatic rollback on health probe failure, and integration with Application Health extension. Manual orchestration requires manual instance management. Flexible mode provides middle ground with upgrade policies. Rolling upgrade policy provides zero-downtime deployment with health-based gating. Manual updates lack orchestration. Deletion causes downtime. Rolling upgrades deliver automated, safe application deployment at scale.

Reference: Upgrade policies for scale sets

questions.promptHtml: A storage account must allow CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) requests from web application hosted at https://app.contoso.com to upload blobs via JavaScript. CORS must allow PUT operations but block DELETE from browser clients for security.

options.label: Configure CORS rules on storage account blob service with allowed origin https://app.contoso.com, methods PUT/GET/HEAD, exposed headers, and exclude DELETE
Configure NSG rules on storage account
Use SAS tokens with specific permissions

questions.correctLabel: Configure CORS rules on storage account blob service with allowed origin https://app.contoso.com, methods PUT/GET/HEAD, exposed headers, and exclude DELETE
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Storage CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) rules specify allowed origins, HTTP methods, headers, and exposed headers at service level (blob, file, queue, table). Browsers enforce CORS (preflight OPTIONS request checks server rules). NSG is network-level security (not HTTP CORS). SAS tokens control permissions but don't enable CORS (browser still blocks cross-origin unless CORS configured). CORS configuration enables secure browser-based storage access from web apps.

Reference: CORS support for Azure Storage
